title: Drugs Used in the Treatment of Gout
summary: النقرس هو التهاب مؤلم في المفاصل ناتج عن ترسب بلورات يورات الصوديوم، ويصيب غالباً مفصل إبهام القدم. يُعالج النقرس الحاد بمضادات الالتهاب غير الستيرويدية (NSAIDs) أو الكولشيسين أو الكورتيكوستيرويدات. أما العلاج طويل الأمد فيهدف إلى تخفيض مستوى حمض اليوريك في الدم إلى ما دون 6 ملغ/ديسيلتر باستخدام مثبطات إنزيم أوكسيديز الزانثين كالأللوبيورينول أو الفيبوكسوستات، أو العوامل اليوريكوزورية كالبروبينيسيد. في الحالات الشديدة المقاومة، يُستخدم البيغلوتيكاز وريدياً لتحويل حمض اليوريك إلى ألانتوين أكثر قابلية للذوبان.

key_drugs.name: Indomethacin / Naproxen
key_drugs.note: خط أول في النقرس الحاد - تثبط تخليق البروستاغلاندين

key_drugs.name: Colchicine
key_drugs.note: تثبط تكوين الأنابيب الدقيقة وبالتالي تمنع هجرة العدلات - أفضل في أول 24 ساعة

key_drugs.name: Prednisone
key_drugs.note: عند وجود تعارض مع NSAIDs أو الكولشيسين

key_drugs.name: Allopurinol
key_drugs.note: خط أول في العلاج طويل الأمد - يخفض إنتاج حمض اليوريك

key_drugs.name: Febuxostat
key_drugs.note: بديل Allopurinol - يرفع خطر الأحداث القلبية الوعائية

key_drugs.name: Probenecid
key_drugs.note: يثبط ناقل URAT1 في الأنابيب الكلوية - يزيد إفراز حمض اليوريك

key_drugs.name: Pegloticase
key_drugs.note: للحالات المقاومة - يحوّل حمض اليوريك إلى ألانتوين - يُعطى وريدياً كل أسبوعين

key_drugs.name: Canakinumab / Anakinra
key_drugs.note: يُثبط الانترلوكين-1 - للحالات التي يتعارض فيها العلاج التقليدي

mnemonics.title: علاج النقرس الحاد: CIA
mnemonics.body:
C = Colchicine
I = Indomethacin (NSAIDs)
A = (Cortico)steroids
الترتيب: NSAIDs أولاً، ثم Colchicine، ثم Steroids عند الحاجة

mnemonics.title: الأللوبيورينول: 'عدو اليورات'
mnemonics.body: تخيّل إنزيم Xanthine Oxidase كـ'مصنع' ينتج حمض اليوريك - الأللوبيورينول يُوقف هذا المصنع تماماً. Allopurinol = 'يوقف الإنتاج'. تذكّر: كلمة 'Allo' تعني 'ضد' = ضد اليوريك.

mnemonics.title: Probenecid يفتح 'باب' الكلية
mnemonics.body: تخيّل الكلية كسجن، وحمض اليوريك سجين. Probenecid يفتح الباب ويطرد السجين خارجاً في البول. كل مرة تسمع Probenecid تذكّر: 'يطرد اليوريك من الكلية'.

mnemonics.title: Pegloticase: محوّل اليوريك
mnemonics.body: Pegloticase = Peg (وتد) + loticase. كأنك تُبدّل شكل حمض اليوريك بضغطة - من مادة صعبة الإذابة إلى ألانتوين سهل الإفراز. يُعطى وريداً كل أسبوعين للحالات الشديدة المقاومة.

mnemonics.title: تأثيرات Colchicine الجانبية: DNA
mnemonics.body:
D = Diarrhea (إسهال - الأكثر شيوعاً)
N = Nausea/Vomiting (غثيان وقيء)
A = Aplasia (كبت نقي العظم)
تذكّر: Colchicine تؤثر على الخلايا السريعة الانقسام

flashcards.front: What is the definition of Gout?
flashcards.back: A painful joint inflammation caused by deposition of monosodium urate crystals, diagnosed by crystal analysis of synovial fluid

flashcards.front: Which joint is most commonly affected in acute gout?
flashcards.back: The first metatarsophalangeal joint (first MTP joint of the big toe)

flashcards.front: What is the difference between primary and secondary gout?
flashcards.back: Primary: genetic and metabolic factors (90%) — Secondary: other diseases or drugs (e.g., diuretics)

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Colchicine?
flashcards.back: Inhibits microtubule formation, thereby preventing neutrophil migration to the site of inflammation

flashcards.front: When is Colchicine most effective?
flashcards.back: Within the first 24 hours of the onset of an acute gout attack

flashcards.front: What are the common adverse effects of Colchicine?
flashcards.back: Diarrhea, nausea and vomiting, bone marrow suppression

flashcards.front: What is the goal of urate-lowering therapy (ULT) for gout?
flashcards.back: To reduce serum uric acid below 6 mg/dL to prevent crystal deposition and dissolve tophi

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Allopurinol and Febuxostat?
flashcards.back: Both inhibit the enzyme xanthine oxidase, reducing the conversion of hypoxanthine and xanthine to uric acid

flashcards.front: What are the serious adverse effects of Allopurinol?
flashcards.back: Skin rash that may progress to Stevens-Johnson syndrome, hepatotoxicity, GI disturbance

flashcards.front: Why is Febuxostat preferred over Allopurinol in some cases?
flashcards.back: When Allopurinol is not tolerated or ineffective — however, Febuxostat increases the risk of cardiovascular events

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Probenecid?
flashcards.back: Inhibits the URAT1 transporter in renal tubules, reducing uric acid reabsorption and increasing its excretion in urine

flashcards.front: What are the adverse effects of Probenecid?
flashcards.back: Uric acid kidney stones, GI disturbance, skin rash

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Pegloticase?
flashcards.back: A recombinant uricase enzyme that converts uric acid to allantoin, which is more soluble and easily excreted

flashcards.front: For whom is Pegloticase used?
flashcards.back: For severe chronic refractory gout in patients who do not respond to or cannot tolerate Allopurinol/Febuxostat

flashcards.front: What is the route of administration of Pegloticase and what are its main adverse effects?
flashcards.back: IV infusion every two weeks — adverse effects: infusion reactions, anaphylaxis requiring monitoring

flashcards.front: When are IL-1 inhibitors (Canakinumab/Anakinra) used in gout?
flashcards.back: When NSAIDs, Colchicine, and corticosteroids are contraindicated, ineffective, or not tolerated

flashcards.front: What is the difference between acute and chronic gout treatment?
flashcards.back: Acute: NSAIDs/Colchicine/Steroids to stop the attack — Chronic: ULT (Allopurinol/Febuxostat/Probenecid) to prevent future attacks

flashcards.front: What are tophi in gout?
flashcards.back: Subcutaneous nodules formed from monosodium urate crystals in a matrix of lipids and proteins — appear in chronic gout

flashcards.front: What causes gout?
flashcards.back: Deposition of uric acid crystals in joints due to hyperuricemia (overproduction or underexcretion).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Colchicine?
flashcards.back: Inhibits microtubule polymerization → reduces neutrophil migration and phagocytosis of crystals.

flashcards.front: What is the most prominent side effect of Colchicine?
flashcards.back: Diarrhea and gastrointestinal disturbance.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Allopurinol?
flashcards.back: Inhibits xanthine oxidase → reduces uric acid production.

flashcards.front: Why is Allopurinol not started during an acute attack?
flashcards.back: It may worsen or prolong the attack by mobilizing urate.

flashcards.front: What is Rasburicase?
flashcards.back: A urate oxidase enzyme that converts uric acid to a soluble compound — used in tumor lysis syndrome.

flashcards.front: What is the important drug interaction of Allopurinol?
flashcards.back: Inhibits metabolism of Azathioprine/6-MP → toxicity (reduce their doses).

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Probenecid?
flashcards.back: Uricosuric agent that inhibits renal urate reabsorption → increases uric acid excretion.

mcqs.q: A patient has been suffering from an acute gout attack for 12 hours. What is the best treatment in this case?
mcqs.explanation: Colchicine is most effective when given within the first 24 hours of an acute attack. It inhibits neutrophil migration by blocking microtubule formation.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism of action of Allopurinol in chronic gout?
options: Increases uric acid excretion in the kidney
mcqs.explanation: Allopurinol inhibits xanthine oxidase, preventing the conversion of hypoxanthine and xanthine to uric acid, thereby reducing its production.

mcqs.q: A patient with chronic gout cannot tolerate Allopurinol. What is the most appropriate alternative for lowering uric acid?
mcqs.explanation: Febuxostat is another xanthine oxidase inhibitor used as an alternative when Allopurinol is not tolerated or ineffective.

mcqs.q: What are the characteristic adverse effects of Colchicine?
options: Diarrhea, nausea, and bone marrow suppression
mcqs.explanation: Colchicine mainly causes diarrhea, nausea and vomiting, and bone marrow suppression at high doses.

mcqs.q: Which of the following drugs works by inhibiting the URAT1 transporter in renal tubules?
mcqs.explanation: Probenecid (a uricosuric agent) inhibits the URAT1 transporter, reducing uric acid reabsorption and increasing its urinary excretion.

mcqs.q: A patient with severe chronic gout refractory to both Allopurinol and Febuxostat. What is the treatment option?
mcqs.explanation: Pegloticase (IV) is reserved for severe refractory gout not responding to standard treatments. Given every two weeks, it converts uric acid to allantoin.

mcqs.q: What is the therapeutic target for serum uric acid in gout patients under treatment?
mcqs.explanation: The goal is to reduce uric acid to below 6 mg/dL (below 5 in severe cases) to prevent crystal deposition and dissolve tophi.

mcqs.q: What are the serious adverse effects of Allopurinol?
mcqs.explanation: Allopurinol can cause a skin rash that progresses to the serious Stevens-Johnson syndrome, as well as hepatotoxicity and GI disturbance.

mcqs.q: In which situation are corticosteroids used for acute gout?
options: When NSAIDs and Colchicine are contraindicated or inappropriate
mcqs.explanation: Corticosteroids are used when there are contraindications or intolerance to NSAIDs and Colchicine. They may be given intra-articularly or systemically.

mcqs.q: A patient with gout is starting ULT. Why is Colchicine given prophylactically at the beginning?
options: To prevent gout flares that may occur when ULT is initiated
To enhance the efficacy of Allopurinol
To reduce the adverse effects of Probenecid
mcqs.explanation: When ULT is started, fluctuations in uric acid levels may trigger acute flares. Colchicine (0.5–1 mg/day) is given prophylactically for 3–6 months.

mcqs.q: Allopurinol is not started during an acute attack because it:
options: May worsen or prolong the attack
mcqs.explanation: Mobilization of urate worsens the attack.

high_yield.point: النوبة الحادة: NSAIDs أو Colchicine أو الجلوكوكورتيكويد. لا تبدأ خافضات اليوريك أثناء النوبة.
Colchicine يثبّط البلمرة الأنبوبية الدقيقة (microtubules) → يقلل هجرة العدلات.
Allopurinol و Febuxostat يثبّطان xanthine oxidase (يقللان إنتاج اليوريك).
Probenecid uricosuric يزيد طرح اليوريك (يُمنع مع حصوات/قصور كلوي).
